name: azure-firewall
title: Azure Firewall — Managed Appliance
summary: Creates an Azure Firewall (Standard or Premium) with a firewall policy, one rule collection group carrying network/application/DNAT collections, a Standard public IP, optional zone deployment, and a CanNotDelete management lock (on by default).

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory tags.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' tag.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' tag.

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional tags merged beneath the mandatory tag set. Mandatory tags win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: resource_group_name
module.description: Name of the existing resource group for the firewall.

module.name: firewall_subnet_id
module.description: ID of the AzureFirewallSubnet (must be named exactly 'AzureFirewallSubnet', minimum /26). Supplied by ID — no framework dependency is implied.

module.name: sku_tier
module.description: Firewall SKU tier: Standard or Premium (Premium adds TLS inspection, IDPS, URL filtering).

module.name: zones
module.description: Availability zones for the firewall (e.g., [\"1\", \"2\", \"3\"]). Empty = regional (non-zonal).

module.name: threat_intelligence_mode
module.description: Threat-intel mode on the policy: Off, Alert, or Deny.

module.name: dns_proxy_enabled
module.description: Enable DNS proxy on the firewall policy (required when using FQDNs in network rules).

module.name: dns_servers
module.description: Custom DNS servers the firewall uses for resolution when DNS proxy is enabled. Empty = Azure DNS.

module.name: network_rule_collections
module.type: map(object({ priority = number action = string rules = map(object({ protocols = list(string) source_addresses = optional(list(string), []) destination_addresses = optional(list(string), []) destination_fqdns = optional(list(string), []) destination_ports = list(string) })) }))
module.description: L3/L4 network rule collections keyed by logical name.

module.name: application_rule_collections
module.type: map(object({ priority = number action = string rules = map(object({ source_addresses = optional(list(string), []) destination_fqdns = optional(list(string), []) protocols = optional(list(object({ type = string port = number })), [{ type = "Https", port = 443 }]) })) }))
module.description: L7 application (FQDN) rule collections keyed by logical name.

module.name: nat_rule_collections
module.type: map(object({ priority = number rules = map(object({ protocols = list(string) source_addresses = list(string) destination_ports = list(string) translated_address = string translated_port = number })) }))
module.description: DNAT rule collections keyed by logical name. Destination address is always the firewall public IP.

module.name: enable_management_lock
module.description: Apply a CanNotDelete management lock to the firewall (governance/resource-locks).

outputs.name: firewall_ids
outputs.description: Map with single key 'firewall' to the Azure Firewall ID.

outputs.name: firewall_private_ip
outputs.description: Private IP of the firewall — use as next_hop_in_ip_address in UDRs that steer traffic through the firewall.

outputs.name: firewall_public_ip
outputs.description: Public IP of the firewall (DNAT destination).

outputs.name: rule_ids
outputs.description: Map with single key 'rule_collection_group' to the rule collection group ID (Azure manages rules inside the group, not as standalone resources).
